title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski, Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, hit a walk-off single in the 10th inning to lead the Braves past the Red Sox 3-2.
The Red Sox, now 18-26, tied the game with a solo homer by Marcelo Mayer but struggled with runners in scoring position.
Red Sox starter Connelly Early allowed two solo homers over five innings, striking out six but was pulled after 86 pitches.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: A reunion between Stefon Diggs and the Patriots is unlikely due to an imminent trade for A.J. Brown.
The Patriots' salary cap constraints make it difficult to afford Diggs alongside A.J. Brown and Romeo Doubs.
Stefon Diggs and the Patriots have mutual interest, but financial and roster priorities complicate a reunion.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police seek 15-year-old Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, last seen Tuesday near 965 Blue Hill Ave in Dorchester.
LaGrant-Gomes is described as 5'8", with a thin build, brown eyes, and black hair; he frequents Columbia Road and Downtown Crossing.
Anyone with information should contact District B-3 detectives at 617-343-4712 or submit anonymous tips via CrimeStoppers.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, accused of shooting girlfriend's mother and another man in Dorchester on Mother's Day.
Broomes faces charges including assault with intent to murder and unlawful firearm possession.
The victims, both 42, suffered severe and non-life-threatening injuries; investigation is ongoing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: A lineup of former Red Sox players in 2026 includes Mookie Betts, Xander Bogaerts, and Kyle Schwarber.
Five former Red Sox starting pitchers currently have a combined ERA of 2.74 in 39 starts.
Dominic Smith has emerged as a surprise star, batting over .350 as of May 15, 2026.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roman Anthony played catch after shedding his wrist brace, but hasn't resumed swinging a bat yet.
The Red Sox outfielder, injured since May 4, might begin hitting soon according to interim manager Chad Tracy.
Anthony batted .385 with a .467 on-base percentage in early May before his wrist injury.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep accidentally drove into the BrightPath daycare in Norwood, causing temporary closure for structural assessment.
No injuries occurred among children, staff, or the driver after the vehicle hit the daycare's front entrance.
The crash, caused by failure to put the Jeep in park, led to moderate building damage, with investigation ongoing.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict the New England Patriots will finish with a 10-7 record for the 2026 season.
The Patriots face a tougher schedule in 2026, with key games against Seattle, Denver, and Chicago.
Despite offseason improvements, experts believe New England will have fewer wins compared to their 14-3 season in 2025.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Marty, Life is Short' starring Martin Short is now streaming on Netflix, directed by Lawrence Kasdan.
'Pop Culture Jeopardy!' hosted by Colin Jost is streaming its second season on Netflix.
'Mars Attacks!' a Tim Burton sci-fi comedy is available for free on YouTube and Plex.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Wu's $4.9 billion budget faces potential rejection by Boston City Council due to proposed cuts.
Councilors can't increase the total spending in the budget, only amend line items before a June 10 deadline.
Mayor warns increasing budget could be fiscally irresponsible; potential rejection may lead to lower budget resubmission.
